historyId: h_v1_EARLY_Excessive phone or social media use
messages: Preparing for a long drive next weekend—what basic car maintenance should I do before leaving?
Explain blockchain consensus in a few plain sentences—I'm trying to explain it to a friend.

Back to the phone issue: earlier I said one category took up the lion's share of my daily use. What habit changes would most directly shrink that dominant category while still letting me get urgent messages?
If I aim to halve that biggest portion of my phone time over the next four weeks, what weekly targets and simple tracking method would make that goal measurable?
Also, I mentioned a particular late-night window when I tend to doomscroll—what realistic replacement routine could I slot into that specific time so I avoid the bedtime-scroll trap?

Finally, give a three-step plan focused on reducing the single biggest contributor I described earlier, with measurable checkpoints and minimal disruption to my study sessions—please don't restate my original statistics, just the concrete steps.
